preamble: The shift from the neutral schwa [ə] to the mid-back rounded vowel [o] is a form of re-stressing or vowel restoration. In languages with heavy vowel reduction, the schwa functions as a placeholder for various underlying full vowels; when the stress shifts back to the syllable, the original quality (often a back-rounded one) is restored.

examples.note: Vowel restoration: the schwa re-opens to a full back vowel when the stress shifts to the first syllable.
Akanje restoration: the reduced pre-tonic vowel returns to its full [o] quality when the stress shifts onto it in the accusative case.
